info.title: Organization | Basic operations
info.description:
BILL is a cloud-based cash flow management system for an organization's Accounts Payable (AP) and Accounts Receivable (AR) automation.
 * **AP**: Automate workflows to pay vendors bills at scale for the goods and services they provide. Pay vendors with a wide range of payment methods. Generate vendor credits, bill credits, and payment disbursement reports.
 * **AR**: Automate workflows to get paid by customers at scale for the goods and services you provide. Generate and send invoices on a schedule, generate customer and invoice credits, and seamlessly charge authorized customers.
 * **Organization**: Manage your bank accounts (for sending and receiving payments), users (with user roles), and sync with your choice of accounting software.

Integrate with the BILL API to send and receive payments for your business. See [Support Center](https://help.bill.com/hc/en-us) for resources about using the BILL web app.

post.description:
Read the organization details for the provided organization ID.

**Note**: The currently signed in user must be associated with the organization to retrieve this information.

### What is an organization?
The `Organization` object represents a unique legal entity i.e. a company. Other business objects such as `User` and `BankAccount` fall under the `Organization` object.

A user can be associated with multiple organizations. An organization can have multiple users performing different operations.

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

post.description:
Get a document that is attached to a BILL object. With `/v2/GetDocumentPages.json`, you can retrieve documents attached to bills and vendor credits.

**Note**: Currently, `/v2/GetDocumentPages.json` does not support retrieving documents attached to an AR invoice.

In your request, set `id` as the BILL object ID. For example, set `id` as the bill ID for retrieving a document that is attached to the bill.

### BILL object without an attachment
In the response, `fileUrl` is set as `null`.

### BILL object with a single text attachment
In the response, a `fileUrl` value is generated, which includes an `id`. Use the returned `id` in a subsequent GET request for downloading the text attachment. Note that the attachment must be in the `.txt` format.

```
curl --request GET 'https://api-stage.bill.com/HtmlServlet?id={fileUrl_id}'
--header 'sessionId: {session_id}'
```

### BILL object with a multi-page attachment
In the response, a `fileUrl` is generated and `numPages` is the number of pages in the attachment. Use the returned `id` in a subsequent GET request for downloading the attachment. For a single-page attachment, set `pageNumber` as `1` in your GET request.

```
curl –-request GET 'https://api-stage.bill.com/is/BillImageServlet?entityId={fileUrl_id}&pageNumber={page_number}'
--header 'sessionId: {session_id}'
```

**Note**: Documents are downloaded in the PNG format.

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

post.description:
Get a document attached to a bill. In your request, set `id` as the bill ID. The value begins with `00n`.

In the response, a `fileUrl` value is generated, which includes an `id`. Use the returned `id` in a subsequent GET request for downloading the bill attachment. Set `--output` as a file name and extension. For example, `bill-attachment.pdf`.

**Note**: Set the correct file extension in your GET request. For example, you cannot download a PNG file as a PDF.

```
curl 'https://api-stage.bill.com/DownloadBillDocumentServlet?id={fileUrl_id}&billId={bill_id}&pageNumber={page_number}' \
--output {file_name} \
--header 'sessionId: {session_id}'
```

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

post.description:
Add a note to a BILL object, such as a bill or invoice.

By default, notes are visible only amongst users in your organization. In your request, set isPublic as true to share notes publicly amongst customers and vendors in the BILL network. Set objectId as the BILL object to add a note. For example, set objectId as a bill ID to add a note to the bill.

You can add notes to a range of BILL objects.
* Vendors
* Bills
* Vendor Credits
* Customers
* Invoices
* Invoice Credits

post.description:
Create a user object.

### What is a user?
A BILL organization user can perform operations depending on their user role profile (Administrator, Accountant, Approver, Clerk, Payer, Custom).

You can create a new user object with `/v2/Crud/Create/User.json`. The user role `profileId` is required for creating and managing users. Retrieve `id` values for all user role profiles with `/v2/List/Profile.json`.

post.description:
Delete a user object.

In the response, `isActive` is set as `2` (Inactive).

**Note**: You can only assign active users to approve bills or vendor credits. Before deleting an Approver user, make sure that you set another user as an Approver for the bill or vendor credit.

### What is a user?
A BILL organization user can perform operations depending on their user role profile (Administrator, Accountant, Approver, Clerk, Payer, Custom). 

You can create a new user object with `/v2/Crud/Create/User.json`. The user role `profileId` is required for creating and managing users. Retrieve `id` values for all user role profiles with `/v2/List/Profile.json`.

post.description:
Read a user role profile object.

### What is a user role profile?
A user role profile defines roles and permissions assigned to each user role. A user can be assigned only one role. You can set user profiles with the BILL web app.

BILL provides a set of default roles.
* Administrator
* Accountant
* Approver
* Payer
* Clerk
* Auditor
* Custom

See [User roles and permissions](https://help.bill.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000024183) in the BILL Help Center for more information.

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

post.description:
Get the current bank balance in the organization’s default bank account. This bank balance value is synced with your choice of accounting software (Intuit QuickBooks Online, Intuit QuickBooks Desktop, Xero, and Sage Intacct).

In the response, the current `bankBalance` and `bankBalanceDate` is returned only when the bank account is associated with a chart of accounts.

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

post.description:
Set the current bank balance value that is synced with your choice of accounting software (Intuit QuickBooks Online, Intuit QuickBooks Desktop, Xero, and Sage Intacct).

**Note**: The current `bankBalance` and `bankBalanceDate` is set only when the bank account is associated with a chart of accounts.

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

post.description:
Read a money movement object. 

### What is money movement?
Money movement in your BILL organization refers to funds transferred to pay a bill or to receive a payment for an invoice.

When funds are sent or received in the bank account, BILL uses money movement information to reconcile the bank account balance and accounting software journal records.

BILL transfers funds with `Money In Clearing` and `Money Out Clearing` accounts. A journal record entry tracks each money movement entry.

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

id.description: BILL-generated ID of the organization. The value begins with `008`.

Response status.
* `0`: Success response
* `1`: Error response. Only `error_code` and `error_message` is returned in `response_data`.

* `Error`: Error response. Only `error_code` and `error_message` is returned in `response_data`.

timezoneId.description:
Organization time zone.
* `1`: Hawaii–Aleutian Standard Time (HST)
* `2`: Alaska Standard Time (AST)
* `3`: Pacific Standard Time (PST)
* `4`: Mountain Standard Time (MST)
* `5`: Central Standard Time (CST)
* `6`: Eastern Standard Time (EST)
* `7`: Puerto Rico and US Virgin Islands Time (PRT)
* `8`: Argentina Standard Time (AGT)
* `9`: Greenwich Mean Time (GMT)
* `10`: European Central Time (ECT)
* `11`: Egypt Standard Time (ART)
* `12`: Eastern African Time (EAT)
* `13`: India Standard Time (IST)
* `14`: Bangladesh Standard Time (BST)
* `15`: Vietnam Standard Time (VST) 
* `16`: China Standard Time (PRC)
* `17`: Japan Standard Time (JST)
* `18`: Australia Eastern Time (AET)
* `19`: Solomon Standard Time (SST)
* `21`: New Zealand Standard Time (NST)
* `22`: MST7MDT
* `23`: EST5EDT

op.description:
Operator symbol.
* `=`: Equals
* `<`: Less than
* `>`: Greater than
* `!=`: Not equal
* `<=`: Less than or equal to
* `>=`: Greater than or equal to
* `in`: Value in list
* `nin`: Value not in list

asc.description:
Sort order.
* `true`: Ascending order
* `false`: Descending order

nested.description: Set as `true` to include additional nested data in the response

id.description: BILL-generated ID of the organization bank account. The value begins with `bac`. You can filter with this field.

isActive.description:
Object visibility status. Inactive objects are hidden by default in the BILL web app. You can filter with this field.
* `1`: Active
* `2`: Inactive

createdTime.description: Created date and time. You can filter with this field.

updatedTime.description: Updated date and time. You can filter with this field.

usersId.description: BILL-generated ID of the user. You can filter with this field.

chartOfAccountId.description: BILL-generated ID of the chart of accounts. The value begins with `0ca`. This is an accounts tracking object. You can filter with this field.

status.description:
Bank account verification status. You can filter with this field.
* `0`: Not verified
* `1`: Verified
* `2`: Pending
* `3`: Blocked
* `4`: Expired
* `5`: Invalid

primaryAP.description: Set as `true` if this is the primary bank account for Accounts Payable (AP). You can filter with this field.

primaryAR.description: Set as `true` if this is the primary bank account for Accounts Receivable (AR). You can filter with this field.

isSavings.description:
Customer bank account type. You can filter with this field.
* `true`: Savings account
* `false`: Checking account

isPersonalAcct.description:
Customer bank account owner type. You can filter with this field.
* `true`: Personal account
* `false`: Business account

autoAdminAuth.description: Set as `true` if `Administrator` users are automatically nominated to use this bank account. You can filter with this field.

dateEstablished.description: Bank account established date. The value is in the YYYY-MM-DD format. You can filter with this field.

verificationLevel.description:
Bank account verification level. You can filter with this field.
* `0`: None
* `1`: Relaxed fraud verification
* `2`: Strict fraud verification
* `3`: Verified

bankBalance.description: Bank account balance. You can filter with this field.

verifyStatus.description:
Bank account verification status. You can filter with this field.
* `0`: ACH verified
* `1`: Instant Transfer availability check in progress
* `2`: Instant Transfer approval in progress
* `3`: Instant Transfer verified. If Instant Transfer fails, status reverts to ACH verified.

id.description: BILL-generated ID of the `MoneyMovement` object. The value begins with `mmt`.

name.description: ACH confirmation ID visible in the bank statement

type.description:
Money movement function.
* `1`: Payable
* `2`: Receivable
* `3`: Wallet funding
* `4`: Wallet withdrawal

amount.description: Money movement amount. The value is negative if funds are withdrawn from the bank.

processDate.description: Money movement processing date. The value is in the YYYY-MM-DD format.

status.description:
Money movement status.
* `0`: Sent
* `1`: Confirmed
* `2`: Voided

amount.description: Money movement item amount. The value is negative if funds are withdrawn from the bank.

id.description: BILL-generated ID of the organization card account. The value begins with `cat`.

cardType.description:
Card type.
* `1`: Visa
* `2`: MasterCard
* `3`: DiscoverNetwork
* `4`: AmericanExpress

status.description:
Card account verification status.
* `0`: Not verified
* `1`: Verified
* `2`: Pending
* `3`: Blocked
* `4`: Expired
* `5`: Invalid

last4.description: Last four digits of the card number

canFund.description: Set as `true` if the card account is used for funding

canDisburse.description: Set as `true` if the card account is used for disbursements

isActive.description:
Object visibility status. Inactive objects are hidden by default in the BILL web app.
* `1`: Active
* `2`: Inactive

primaryAP.description: Set as `true` if this is the primary card account for Accounts Payable (AP)

primaryAR.description: Set as `true` if this is the primary card account for Accounts Receivable (AR)
